method_atom: A path-integral (functional integration over trajectories) computation of the full probability distribution of a time-integrated fluctuating quantity in a linear stochastic system that includes inertia, i.e. second-order Langevin dynamics with damping and random forcing held in a nonequilibrium steady state. The approach yields the distribution in different reference frames and proves an asymptotic symmetry relation (large-deviation fluctuation theorem) for that quantity from arbitrary initial conditions, while exposing finite-time transients including damped oscillatory behavior once the inertial parameter exceeds a nonzero critical value.
object_atom: The work needed to sustain a massive Brownian particle that is dragged through a thermal medium (a mass-bearing diffusing particle pulled at constant velocity) in a nonequilibrium steady state, compared in the laboratory and comoving frames.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the trajectory path-integral calculation of a time-integrated observable's distribution for inertial linear stochastic dynamics and its asymptotic fluctuation-symmetry theorem; the domain-specific object is the dragged massive Brownian particle and the work sustaining its steady state.
